basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Munāfiqūn — “the Hypocrites” — unmasks those who come to the Prophet with the profession of faith on their lips and its denial in their hearts: “We bear witness that you are the Messenger of God” — and God bears witness that they lie, not about Muḥammad being His Messenger, but about their claim to believe it. Qushayrī opens the sūra with a meditation on truthfulness (ṣidq) flowing through the whole divine Name: the servant's sincerity in his words, then in his deeds, then in his character, then in his states, then even in his breath; truthful in speech is to speak only upon proof; truthful in deed is that innovation hold no power over him; truthful in character is never to regard his own goodness with a self-satisfied eye. And he settles in one stroke what hypocrisy is: the truthfulness of the saying profits nothing when the ugliness of the state belies it. He describes the hypocrites as empty shells and propped-up timbers: mere silhouettes and moulds, with no kernel or reality behind them — the hollow nut, bright outside, is but a children's toy; what counts are the realities of the spirits, not the appearances of the bodies. The sūra culminates in its warning to the believers: let neither wealth nor children distract you from the remembrance of God — for if you are for God, God is for you.

versets.ar: إِذَا جَاءَكَ الْمُنَافِقُونَ قَالُوا نَشْهَدُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُهُ ۖ وَاللَّهُ يَشْهَدُ إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ لَكَاذِبُونَ
versets.tr: idhā jāʾaka l-munāfiqūna qālū nashhadu innaka la-rasūlu llāhi wa-llāhu yaʿlamu innaka la-rasūluhu wa-llāhu yashhadu inna l-munāfiqīna la-kādhibūna
versets.trad: When the hypocrites come to you, they say: “We bear witness that you are the Messenger of God!” God knows that you are His Messenger, and God bears witness that the hypocrites are liars.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unties the paradox: God refutes them, yet not in the content of their witness — for Muḥammad truly is the Messenger of God. Their lie is not in the attestation itself; their tongue, outwardly, has spoken truly. The lie lies in their claim to be sincere and faithful to you — hidden in the recess of the heart. Hence the verdict: “the truthfulness of the saying profits nothing when the ugliness of the state belies it” (ṣidq al-qāla).

mots.def: munāfiq: one whose outside belies his inside; from nafaq, the burrow with two exits.

mots.sens: you are indeed the Messenger of God

mots.def: kādhib: liar; opposite of ṣādiq, the truthful.

versets.ar: اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمَانَهُمْ جُنَّةً فَصَدُّوا عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ سَاءَ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: ittakhadhū aymānahum junnatan fa-ṣaddū ʿan sabīli llāhi innahum sāʾa mā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: They have taken their oaths as a shield, and so they have turned away from the path of God. Evil indeed is what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: The oath serves them as a shield (junna): they cover themselves with their profession to hide their denial. Qushayrī: they veiled themselves behind their confession, then uncovered themselves through their hypocrisy — their veils fell, they unmasked themselves, and they tasted the bitterness of their own states. The mask always falls of itself in the end.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ي م ن

mots.def: junna: that which protects, veils and hides; same root as jinn (the hidden).

mots.racine: ص د د / س ب ل
mots.sens: they turned from the path of God
mots.def: ṣadda: to turn away; sabīl: way, path.

mots.racine: س و أ / ع م ل

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ آمَنُوا ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا فَطُبِعَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَفْقَهُونَ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-annahum āmanū thumma kafarū fa-ṭubiʿa ʿalā qulūbihim fa-hum lā yafqahūna
versets.trad: That is because they believed, then disbelieved; so a seal was set upon their hearts, and they no longer understand.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the image of the extinguished light: they had lit themselves at the glow of being answered, but the ray of felicity did not spread over them; their light was snuffed out by the force of deprivation, and they remained in the darkness of the antecedent allotment, by the decree of wretchedness. The seal (ṭabʿ) is no blind fate: it is the hardening of a heart that knew the light and refused it.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ك ف ر
mots.def: āmana: to believe; kafara: to deny, reject.
mots.gram: two perfect verbs joined by thumma

mots.racine: ط ب ع / ق ل ب
mots.sens: a seal was set upon their hearts

versets.ar: وَإِذَا رَأَيْتَهُمْ تُعْجِبُكَ أَجْسَامُهُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَقُولُوا تَسْمَعْ لِقَوْلِهِمْ ۖ كَأَنَّهُمْ خُشُبٌ مُّسَنَّدَةٌ ۖ يَحْسَبُونَ كُلَّ صَيْحَةٍ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ هُمُ الْعَدُوُّ فَاحْذَرْهُمْ ۚ قَاتَلَهُمُ اللَّهُ ۖ أَنَّىٰ يُؤْفَكُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā raʾaytahum tuʿjibuka ajsāmuhum wa-in yaqūlū tasmaʿ li-qawlihim ka-annahum khushubun musannadatun yaḥsabūna kulla ṣayḥatin ʿalayhim humu l-ʿaduwwu fa-ḥdharhum qātalahumu llāhu annā yuʾfakūna
versets.trad: When you see them, their bodies please you; and if they speak, you listen to their words. They are like propped-up timbers. They reckon every cry to be against them. They are the enemy, so beware of them. May God destroy them! How perverted they are!
versets.commentaire: The sūra's master image, per Qushayrī: they are mere silhouettes and moulds; behind them there is neither kernel nor reality — the hollow nut, bright outside, is but a children's toy. From which he draws the Sufi rule: what counts are the realities of the spirits, not the appearances of the bodies. Their dread — “every cry is against them” — comes from this: they find no solace in their Lord, and no support in any other than Him: the coward trembles because he has no one to lean upon.

mots.racine: ع ج ب / ج س م
mots.def: ajsām: bodies, frames; aʿjaba: to please, impress.

mots.racine: خ ش ب / س ن د
mots.def: khushub: pieces of wood, beams; musannada: propped, leaning against a wall.

mots.racine: ح س ب / ص ي ح
mots.sens: they reckon every cry against them

mots.racine: ع د و / ح ذ ر
mots.sens: they are the enemy, so beware of them
mots.def: ʿaduww: enemy; ḥadhira: to beware, take care.
ufika: to be deviated from truth, perverted.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا يَسْتَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ لَوَّوْا رُءُوسَهُمْ وَرَأَيْتَهُمْ يَصُدُّونَ وَهُم مُّسْتَكْبِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā qīla lahum taʿālaw yastaghfir lakum rasūlu llāhi lawwaw ruʾūsahum wa-raʾaytahum yaṣuddūna wa-hum mustakbirūna
versets.trad: When it is said to them: “Come, the Messenger of God will ask forgiveness for you!”, they turn their heads away, and you see them draw back, swollen with pride.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they listen to what is said to them with haughtiness, showing that they can do without your asking forgiveness — so leave them: counsel has no hold upon them. And he adds, terribly: nothing will sober them from their drunkenness but the burning of the punishment they will meet. Pride (istikbār) is the one wall the offered mercy cannot cross, for it refuses even the outstretched hand.

mots.sens: he will ask forgiveness for you

mots.racine: ل و ي / ر أ س
mots.def: lawwā: to twist, turn aside; ruʾūs: heads.
mustakbir: one who deems himself great, haughty.

versets.ar: سَوَاءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَسْتَغْفَرْتَ لَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ لَن يَغْفِرَ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: sawāʾun ʿalayhim astaghfarta lahum am lam tastaghfir lahum lan yaghfira llāhu lahum inna llāha lā yahdī l-qawma l-fāsiqīna
versets.trad: It is the same for them whether you ask forgiveness for them or do not: God will not forgive them. Truly, God does not guide the perverse people.
themes: the equality of the two outcomes
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the prior knowledge has already settled it — this verse decrees no new severity, it discloses what was known from eternity. As long as their obstinacy (iṣrār) persists, intercession remains without effect: it is not God who closes Himself off, but the perverse one (fāsiq) who has placed himself outside the way of guidance.

mots.sens: it is the same for them
whether you ask forgiveness or not

mots.def: fāsiq: one who departs from obedience, dissolute.

versets.ar: هُمُ الَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ لَا تُنفِقُوا عَلَىٰ مَنْ عِندَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ حَتَّىٰ يَنفَضُّوا ۗ وَلِلَّهِ خَزَائِنُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَلَٰكِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ لَا يَفْقَهُونَ
versets.tr: humu lladhīna yaqūlūna lā tunfiqū ʿalā man ʿinda rasūli llāhi ḥattā yanfaḍḍū wa-lillāhi khazāʾinu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-lākinna l-munāfiqīna lā yafqahūna
versets.trad: They are the ones who say: “Spend nothing on those who are with the Messenger of God, so that they may disperse from him.” Yet to God belong the treasures of the heavens and the earth, but the hypocrites do not understand.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: as though they were tied to secondary causes, veiled from the contemplation of the decree, ignorant of who governs the days. They think to starve the believers by cutting off provisions, forgetting that the treasures of the heavens and the earth belong to God. And he brings it back to the Prophet: it is neither your self-sufficiency, O Muḥammad, nor your companions' that feeds you — but the Provider (al-Rāziq), it is He who sustains you. He reports that masters like al-Junayd and al-Shiblī would answer with this verse when asked: “From where do you eat?”

mots.sens: the treasures of the heavens and the earth

versets.ar: يَقُولُونَ لَئِن رَّجَعْنَا إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ لَيُخْرِجَنَّ الْأَعَزُّ مِنْهَا الْأَذَلَّ ۚ وَلِلَّهِ الْعِزَّةُ وَلِرَسُولِهِ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَلَٰكِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: yaqūlūna la-in rajaʿnā ilā l-madīnati la-yukhrijanna l-aʿazzu minhā l-adhalla wa-lillāhi l-ʿizzatu wa-li-rasūlihi wa-lil-muʾminīna wa-lākinna l-munāfiqīna lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: They say: “If we return to Medina, the mightier will surely drive out the weaker from it.” But might belongs to God, and to His Messenger, and to the believers — though the hypocrites do not know.
themes: the reversal of strong and weak
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their error was in misnaming who was the mightier and who the lowlier — they imagined the mightier were the hypocrites and the lowlier the Muslims; but it is the reverse. And he distributes glory (ʿizza) as three rays of a single source: to God the glory of divinity, to the Messenger the glory of prophethood, to the believers the glory of sainthood (walāya) — and all of it is God's: the glory of the Eternal is His very attribute, that of the Messenger and the believers is of His grace. He concludes: there is no glory but in obedience to God, and no abasement but in disobedience to Him.

mots.racine: ر ج ع / م د ن
mots.def: rajaʿa: to return; al-Madīna: the City.

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ع ز ز / ذ ل ل
mots.sens: the mightier will drive out the lowlier
mots.def: aʿazz: the mightier; adhall: the lowlier.
mots.gram: emphatic lām + emphatic verb + two superlatives

mots.racine: ر س ل / أ م ن
mots.sens: and to His Messenger and the believers

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تُلْهِكُمْ أَمْوَالُكُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُكُمْ عَن ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tulhikum amwālukum wa-lā awlādukum ʿan dhikri llāhi wa-man yafʿal dhālika fa-ulāʾika humu l-khāsirūna
versets.trad: O you who believe, let neither your wealth nor your children distract you from the remembrance of God! Those who do so are the losers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: do not ruin the affairs of your religion on account of your wealth and your children; rather prefer the right of God, and occupy yourself with Him: He will suffice you for the affairs of your world and your children. And the pearl that distills the whole sūra: idhā kunta lillāhi kāna llāhu lak — if you are for God, God is for you. He adds: God's right is what He has charged you with; your own portion He has guaranteed to fulfill for you — so busy yourself with what you are charged with, not with what has been taken off your hands.

mots.def: alhā: to distract, divert by amusement.

mots.racine: م و ل / و ل د

mots.def: dhikr: remembrance, mention, invocation of God.
khāsir: one who loses, who is bankrupt.

versets.ar: وَأَنفِقُوا مِن مَّا رَزَقْنَاكُم مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ فَيَقُولَ رَبِّ لَوْلَا أَخَّرْتَنِي إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ فَأَصَّدَّقَ وَأَكُن مِّنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-anfiqū min mā razaqnākum min qabli an yaʾtiya aḥadakumu l-mawtu fa-yaqūla rabbi lawlā akhkhartanī ilā ajalin qarībin fa-aṣṣaddaqa wa-akun mina ṣ-ṣāliḥīna
versets.trad: Spend [in alms] of what We have provided you, before death comes to one of you and he says: “My Lord! If only You would grant me a short delay, that I might give alms and be among the righteous.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a cry of awakening: do not be deceived by the peace of your hours; watch for the suddenness of your appointed terms; hold yourselves ready for the departure before you, and do not encamp in the lands of procrastination (taswīf). The dying man's regret, begging “a short delay,” is the very image of the soul that has put off until tomorrow what is lived only today.

mots.racine: ن ف ق / ر ز ق
mots.sens: spend of what We have provided you

mots.ar: مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ
mots.tr: min qabli an yaʾtiya aḥadakumu l-mawt
mots.racine: أ ت ي / م و ت
mots.sens: before death comes to one of you
if only You would grant me a delay

mots.racine: ص د ق / ص ل ح
mots.sens: that I might give alms and be of the righteous
mots.def: taṣaddaqa: to give alms; ṣāliḥ: righteous, upright.

versets.ar: وَلَن يُؤَخِّرَ اللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِذَا جَاءَ أَجَلُهَا ۚ وَاللَّهُ خَبِيرٌ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lan yuʾakhkhira llāhu nafsan idhā jāʾa ajaluhā wa-llāhu khabīrun bimā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: God will not grant any soul a delay once its term has come. And God is fully aware of what you do.
versets.commentaire: The term (ajal) is not deferred: the sūra that opened on the lie of hearts closes on the one truth that never lies — the appointed hour. And God remains khabīr, aware of the most secret of acts: He who knows the inside of the hypocrites knows also the inside of whoever defers his return. The only delay that counts is the one seized before it closes.

mots.racine: أ خ ر / ن ف س
mots.sens: God will not defer any soul
mots.def: akhkhara: to defer; nafs: soul, person.
mots.gram: negation of the future + verb + object

mots.racine: ج ي أ / أ ج ل
خ ب ر / ع م ل
mots.sens: God is aware of what you do
